violations.text: Chicken wings were observed held at 43-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the flip top prep unit. Staff instructed to keep chicken wings in bottom drawer to keep proper temperature. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, wings were kept in unit overnight. PIC voluntarily disposed of item. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/09/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Raw chicken was observed stored over raw burgers that can be raw or undercooked per customers request. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Foods relocated to proper areas at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Pink sauce made on site, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of food at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Various items were observed held between 46-60 degrees Fahrenheit in various refrigeration units throughout the kitchen. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All items left in units over 4 hours voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection by PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken and mushrooms were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep and covered. Person in charge removed food from containers into sheet pans to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The flip top prep unit and the cold drawer unit was observed in poor repair (maintaining ambient temperature above 41 degrees Fahrenheit). Units to not be used until it can be replaced/repaired/serviced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC voluntarily disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024
Strawberries were observed moldy. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2023

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Gravy was placed hot in the walk-in in plastic containers greater than four inches deep. Gravy was relocated to a shallow metal pan with ice wand to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2023

ROP Lobster was observed with thawing with package still in tact. Lobster was still partially frozen. Lobster was cut open. Proper thawing procedures to be reviewed with staff. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of bio-film. Ice machine was turned off to be wash, rinsed, and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2023
